Jon: 	We are going to take questions, and we’re going to do it in a unique
way, I think. Josh said there are so many questions coming in, that the
best way we can handle this is to have him Skype me the individual
questions, and I’m just going to start reading them. That way, we don’t
have to go back and forth, and there’s a ton of lag time.
So, there’s a lot of questions. We’re going to get through as many as
we can. So, Skype me one at a time here, Josh.
First question is from David White, feedback on his USP. “The #1 way
to generate maximum online sales in top class positioning for explosive
profit and growth.”
I would say for “explosive profit and growth,” that would be the only
phrase I would change. Explosive profit and growth. Just put the word
“and” in there, unless “profit growth” is a term that you use.
That’s an awesome USP. It’s perfect.
“Our clients stand out from the crowd. We help them differentiate.
That’s an awesome tip, right there. That’s an awesome thing to avoid.
“Here’s one thing you want to avoid, you want to avoid blending into
the crowd. And the reason why is blah, blah, blah.”
“Our clients are easily found when potential clients search online.”
That’s a huge benefit.
Maximum ROI, huge benefit. David, I think you’re right on track there.
I don’t have any feedback, other than that profit growth line.
Good. Awesome job, David. Awesome job.

What is the benefit of not slamming on your brakes? Well, the benefit
is here’s something that you need to learn right now, in order to

“And, here’s how you brake smoothly: first step, do this; second step,

I’m making up your dream story, because I don’t know the product well

I’m just using the formula. I’m reading right off the screen.

8
one, step 2, step 3. The result of this is going to be an increase in your
business, your clients are going to feel better, etc, etc. And you are
going to feel blank.” And then you do the mini dream story.
“Just imagine it. You walk into your house, you see the certificate right
there on the wall. You know you’ve done your homework. You know
that you’re qualified, and you’re qualified to do it in the comfort of your
own home.”
So, that’s a great tip. Unless that’s your entire business model. In
other words, if your entire product is built around, “I certify people with
in-home pet care certification.” You never want your tip to be your
product. That’s a really, really killer thing to talk about, right now. You
never want your tip to be your product or your service.
I’m making this up. If your service is emptying cat litter, that’s what you
do, you go around to rich people’s houses and empty their cat litter. I
don’t know if anyone has that service or not, but I wouldn’t want to do
it.
But that’s your service. You don’t want to say, “Let me give you a tip.
Have someone else empty your cat litter.” That’s not a tip. That’s
what you do. Okay? So, you have to differentiate your tips from what
you actually do.
So, I’m going to assume that that’s not what you actually do. If it is, I

anyone else has those tip versus what I do scenarios, let me know.
Okay. Josh, I don’t understand what you just Skyped me. Is this all
from Pam?

Okay, yeah, I love the resist the temptation to weigh yourself. Yeah, I

9
can find, as long as there’s no one underneath.” “Throw it in the
ocean. You do not need your scale.”
And then, the how to do it, would be like, “Look, this is the reason why
you want to do this.” You can turn this how to into a reason why.
“So the reason why you don’t want to weigh yourself is because
muscle weighs more than fat. So, if you gain any muscle in this
process, if you gain any water in this process, it’s going to show up on
the scale. And your body weight fluctuates 3 to 5 pounds a day in
water. So, if you weigh yourself at the wrong time, you’re going to go,
‘Oh, I didn’t lose a pound,’ when in reality you’ve lost 3 pounds. And
the only way you can know for sure if you’ve lost weight is to have a
body count done.”
“Now, the result of doing this, of not weighing yourself, is you’re not
going to psych yourself out. Just go by the mirror. Look in the mirror.
That’s all you have to do. I’ll teach you all the techniques to losing the
weight. All you have to do is look in the mirror.”
“So just image this: being free from the scale for the rest of your life.
Who cares what you weigh? It’s all about what you look like.”
That one was really easy, Pam. I cheated because I’m obviously in the
nutrition/diet field. But yeah, you could actually take that one word-for-
word, if you want. But that’s the idea.
Now, I’m seeing a pattern here, Josh. I’m seeing the pattern that the
benefit is what’s holding people up. So, let me clarify this for
everybody.
You want to start with your benefit. The tip, in her case, is an ideal
example. “Stop weighing yourself;” which, by the way, is a great tip.
The only time I ever weigh myself is before a contest. I don’t care,
otherwise. So, don’t weigh yourself.
I have a rule by the way. I go by my belts and my jeans, because they
don’t lie. The scale can lie. Your jeans rarely do. So, it’s pretty
simple.
So, the tip is very valid; stop weighing yourself. But that’s not the
benefit, right? Right? What is the benefit? You have to ask yourself,
“Why am I telling the reader to do this? What is it going to give them,
and why am I telling them to do it?” Those are the first 2 questions.
What does this give the reader? Well in this case, it frees them from
psyching themselves out. It doesn’t allow them to freak out over

-- 9 of 20 --

10
something false. So, what does that give them? Well, it gives them
the power to keep doing their diet.
“Here’s something that will keep you on track with your diet, forever,
and free you from all this guilt that you’re probably feeling. So, the
reason why you’re going to want to take my advice on this tip is
because you’re probably tired of feeling that guilt. And if you don’t do
this, you’re going to psych yourself out.”
So, I pretty much built the reasons in right there. Benefit, why do it,
and the rest of it is pretty simple. So, good one, Pam.
Jonas. “Tip: I teach, in my Massage Your Date course, you’re literally
changing the way a woman thinks about you on a molecular level,
without you even realizing it.” Okay, that is a benefit. That is a benefit.
And it’s a pretty strong benefit.
You could build that into a tip. But what you have to say is like, “Okay,
here’s this one tip that I’m going to give you. And the benefit is it’s
going to change the way a woman thinks about you, without her even
realizing it. And what is it?”
Unfortunately, the tip is your massage course, so that doesn’t work.
Remember, your tip cannot be your product. So, what you want to do
is pull a section out of your product and say, “What is it? I want you to
massage her shoulders in a specific way.”
Okay? It doesn’t matter that the technique works for legs and
whatever else. It doesn’t matter. You’re just going to say, “I want you
to massage her shoulders in a certain way. This changes things on a
molecular structure.” And you tell why. That is true. I don’t know why.
Changes things in energy. You tell how to do it and then you give the
result.
“The result is that you’re literally, that someone’s thinking differently
about you because of the fill-in-the-blank why. “And just image it. Just
a simple shoulder rub, you could actually convince a girl to fall in love
with you.”
See what a lot of people are doing, this is a pattern, Joshua. It’s a
pattern that I think we can actually take to heart and include in our
homework. Never make your tip your product, okay? And always pull
out a component of your product if you’re going to give a tip that’s
based on your product, which is what I just did.
So, Jonas, let me know. Does the example of massaging her
shoulders, like I picked one area, or massaging her neck – I’m picking
an area of the body that guys, guys when they want to give a massage

and they’re on a date, they’re thinking sex, right? Well, you’re teaching

Now, that’s the reason why you want to do it. The tip is to do what? Is

Okay, Ken. Okay, Ken’s tip is, “Here’s a way to make your car look

Okay, I don’t know anything about this, but I’m going to assume that
that is absolutely the truth. So, I’m going to work with this.
So, the benefit. You started off perfectly. Okay, “Here’s an awesome
thing that you’re going to want to do if you want to make your car look

So, that’s why. I just told you why do it. “What is it? Use car mats and

not only being looking nicer, but people are going to enjoy riding in it
more.”
Okay, hopefully that helped, Ken. That’s a really good one, though.
That’s a really good one, because you started off with the benefit. And
you did not take your entire product. So, good job with that. Hope that

Okay, we’re into a USP now. “My service is a blogging platform for

12
Okay, it’s very specific and very good. “The only awesome job blog

“teaches” to shows or reveals. Reveals is even stronger.

times as you can. “The only blog platform that reveals how you can
quickly create unique content, driving targeted buyers to your real
estate business.” I would say, “targeted, hungry buyers.” Again, this is
wordsmithing. We just covered wordsmithing briefly, but go back over

and it’s a perfect one, dude. It’s perfect. Good job.

Okay, so let’s go on the next one, Josh.

So, the tip is set up really well. In other words, “Here’s something you

So, let’s look at the benefit. The benefit is not this. The benefit is not
this. The benefit is a combination of simple method and protection of
your loved ones. Make sense?
So, the benefit. “I’m going to give you a very simple tip on how you
can help protect the people that you love the most in your home,
inexpensively and quickly. You’re going to want to do this; because if
you don’t, there’s actually a very dangerous condition that could be
existing inside your home right now and you don’t know about it, that
could put your loved ones at risk. And what am I talking about?
Talking about having a carbon monoxide and combustion analyst
check your furnace.”
Now, that works if that’s not your entire business model. If that’s your
entire business model, again, that may not work unless you use that as
your last tip and you build into the pitch. That’s the only time a tip as
your product even will ever work, is if you use it as your how-to and
you use that to build into your product.
So, if I used it as a how-to, I’d say something like this: “Now, I’m going
to let you know that one thing I talked about earlier. It’s the one thing
that most people never do, that you have to do if you want to make
sure your loved ones are secure and safe. And it’s simple. It’s a very
simple method, it’s a very simple process. And what it is, is exactly
what we do. We are carbon monoxide and combustion analysts, and
we will come out and check your furnace.” And then you walk right into
the pitch.
You tell them why they want to do it. “The reason you want to do it.
Here’s how we do it. Here’s the results of when we do it,” and that’s it.
So, that works.
Okay, Penny. Sewing a complicated cool pattern easily.” Okay,
Penny, that’s probably not your USP. That’s probably a tip. Okay,
right? Okay, “Sewing a complicated pattern easy.”
Okay, great tip. Okay, Penny’s saying – okay. I’m reading through this

So, again, what is the benefit here? The benefit is exactly what you

Keep drilling down on benefits, people. This is also a really good

Okay. Anthony. “How do you handle a triple guarantee for an injury

based.
The triple guarantee, it’s in the toolbox because it is exactly what a
toolbox is. A toolbox, if anyone has a toolbox at home, do you use all
your tools? No, unless you’re a mechanic or a real car junkie, you
probably don’t use every single one of your tools.
You have tools there just in case you ever need it. So that’s the VSL
toolbox, and we called it that, is in case you need it.

16
If your product – which is rare by the way, Anthony, so I’m glad you

But your guarantee is built within your entire structure. You can say,

I’m assuming that’s what that means, by the way. So, pretty good one

So, again, I’m making “you” and “the,” rather than “nothing” and “a,”

talking to one person. One person.
So, “Use this one technique to get past the gatekeeper and directly to

Okay, next up. We’ll try to rattle through as many of these as we can.
Anthony, “With the big comparison, how do you compare a law firm
with a competitor? We don’t really have a specific, exact competitor
that is well-known, for us to reference ourselves to, without maybe
calling the competitor instead of us.”
You know what you can do? You can just kind use the ubiquitous
competitor. I don’t know if this is the same person that was talking
earlier. Let me see. Yeah, if you’re contingency-based, it’s simple.
The big comparison is, the really big comparison is, “Now, compare us
to a lot of law firms. They’ll charge you when you walk in the door.
They’ll charge you for a first meeting. They’ll charge you whether they
win the case or not. Not us.”
And that’s all you have to do. You don’t have to name a specific
competitor. You can just say, “The competition often charges for
meetings. The competition often does this, that, or the other.” So,
that’s how you do that.
Okay, Glen. “The full tip formula. Relieve your stress of debt, your

Okay, cool, awesome. You just need to write this in sentence form.
So, in other words, instead of, “Relieve your stress of debt, your

So, here’s why you’re going to want to do it. “You can get collection

that’s perfect. You’ve got it. “You can get collection agencies to stop
calling you.” You can just wordsmith this a little bit. “You can get
those jerk collection agencies to stop harassing you by phone calls

Okay, there’s actually 2 tips here. I would say, “Now, this is a 2-part

report them for harassment, and that you know that creditors do not
